page: Amazon Web Services m8g.metal-24xl is a bare-metal general-purpose server powered by the 64-bit ARM architecture. It features 96 dedicated physical cores utilizing the AWS Neoverse-V2 processor model running at 2.8 GHz. The system is equipped with 384.0 GB of DDR5 memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. There are no integrated GPUs or local NVMe storage drives bundled with this instance type. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 40 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the m8g.metal-24xl demonstrates top-tier multi-core performance. It achieves top 10% placement in multi-core stress-ng tests, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, and Geekbench multi-core scores. Memory subsystem performance is also highly rated, with DDR5 memory bandwidth reaching top-tier speeds for both cached and uncached read and write operations. Database operations, such as Redis SET operations and Passmark database tests, also rank in the top 10% tier. However, single-core performance is more moderate, with stress-ng single-core and Geekbench image processing scoring in the average tier.

Given its bare-metal architecture, high core count, and balanced memory-to-vCPU ratio, this server is highly efficient for multi-threaded workloads that scale across physical cores without hyperthreading overhead. It is well-suited for large-scale web serving, database hosting, software compilation, and multi-threaded data decompression. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the ARM-based Graviton architecture provides high resource density and strong multi-core throughput per resource unit, making it a viable alternative to traditional x86 bare-metal deployments for general-purpose enterprise workloads.
description: The Amazon Web Services m8g.metal-24xl is a bare-metal, general-purpose server powered by 96 dedicated ARM64 physical cores using the AWS Neoverse-V2 processor at 2.8 GHz. It features 384.0 GB of DDR5 memory, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core, with a baseline network bandwidth of 40 Gbps. The instance does not include local storage or GPUs. In benchmark testing, the m8g.metal-24xl delivers top-tier multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth performance, ranking in the top 10% for multi-threaded operations, database tasks, and static web serving. Conversely, single-core performance and image processing fall into the average tier. This bare-metal server is highly suited for multi-threaded enterprise workloads, software compilation, database operations, and high-throughput web serving where dedicated physical cores and high memory bandwidth are required.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8g.metal-24xl is a bare-metal ARM64 server featuring 96 dedicated Neoverse-V2 cores, 384 GB of DDR5 memory, and 40 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier multi-core performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8g.metal-24xl is an ARM64 bare-metal server with 96 dedicated cores, 384 GB DDR5 RAM, and top-tier multi-core performance.
tagline: A high-density, bare-metal ARM64 platform delivering exceptional multi-core throughput and balanced DDR5 memory performance for demanding enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 96 dedicated ARM64 physical cores utilizing AWS Neoverse-V2 processors at 2.8 GHz.
384.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory providing 4.0 GB per core.
Top-tier multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and database benchmark performance.
40 Gbps baseline network bandwidth without local storage or GPU accelerators.
Optimized for multi-threaded workloads, software compilation, and database hosting.
